title: Constraint
description: A constraint on an agent's behavior. Constraints define what an agent CANNOT do - boundaries, prohibitions, or limitations.

rule.description: The constraint rule in natural language. Should be clear and unambiguous.

category.description: Category of constraint: content (what to create), process (how to work), access (permissions), communication (messaging), safety (user protection), quality (output standards), style (aesthetic rules), resource (budget/cost)

enforcement.description: How this constraint is enforced. 'runtime' constraints are programmatically checked, 'llm' constraints rely on agent compliance.

severity.description: Severity if constraint is violated. 'critical' violations invalidate all work.

applies_to_artifact_types.description: If specified, constraint only applies when working with these artifact types
